tool_call.name: file_write
tool_call.args_raw: {"path":"/tmp/wd_test_short.md","content":"Rust's ownership model revolutionizes memory safety by eliminating garbage collection overhead entirely. <wd:1>Rather than relying on reference counting or manual deallocation, Rust employs compile-time verification that guarantees memory safety without runtime costs. <wd:2>The system centers around three fundamental rules governing how data lives and moves through your program. <wd:3>First, every variable has a single owner controlling its lifecycle completely. <wd:4>Second when you transfer ownership to another variable the original binding becomes invalidated automatically. <wd:5>This prevents dangling references from accessing freed memory during execution entirely. <wd:6>Third Rust performs automatic cleanup when variables fall out of scope systematically. <wd:7>The language distinguishes between owned values and borrowed references carefully throughout your codebase always. <wd:8>Borrowing creates temporary views allowing read access without transferring ownership permanently anywhere ever. <wd:9>Mutability requires exclusive access ensuring no concurrent modifications cause undefined behavior consistently everywhere. <wd:10>This design enables fearless concurrency where data races become impossible by construction fundamentally forever. <wd:11>Developers gain performance matching C while achieving safety surpassing managed languages significantly today always. <wd:12>The compiler acts as an uncompromising guardian enforcing these rules during compilation rigorously always. <wd:13>Understanding ownership unlocks Rust's full potential for systems programming excellence completely everywhere now."}

args_json.content: Rust's ownership model revolutionizes memory safety by eliminating garbage collection overhead entirely. <wd:1>Rather than relying on reference counting or manual deallocation, Rust employs compile-time verification that guarantees memory safety without runtime costs. <wd:2>The system centers around three fundamental rules governing how data lives and moves through your program. <wd:3>First, every variable has a single owner controlling its lifecycle completely. <wd:4>Second when you transfer ownership to another variable the original binding becomes invalidated automatically. <wd:5>This prevents dangling references from accessing freed memory during execution entirely. <wd:6>Third Rust performs automatic cleanup when variables fall out of scope systematically. <wd:7>The language distinguishes between owned values and borrowed references carefully throughout your codebase always. <wd:8>Borrowing creates temporary views allowing read access without transferring ownership permanently anywhere ever. <wd:9>Mutability requires exclusive access ensuring no concurrent modifications cause undefined behavior consistently everywhere. <wd:10>This design enables fearless concurrency where data races become impossible by construction fundamentally forever. <wd:11>Developers gain performance matching C while achieving safety surpassing managed languages significantly today always. <wd:12>The compiler acts as an uncompromising guardian enforcing these rules during compilation rigorously always. <wd:13>Understanding ownership unlocks Rust's full potential for systems programming excellence completely everywhere now.
